story_clusters.headline: TMC Crisis Deepens as Saayoni Ghosh, Mala Roy Reach Delhi Ahead of Rebels’ Meeting with Speaker
story_clusters.summary: TMC leaders Saayoni Ghosh and Mala Roy traveled to Delhi ahead of a meeting with the Speaker amid an internal crisis in the party. Meanwhile, BJP leaders are set to perform havans to mark Narendra Modi becoming India’s longest-serving prime minister. Separately, Delhi’s chief minister led a cleanliness drive along the Yamuna riverbank with BJP leaders and volunteers participating.

story_clusters.summary: Multiple sources report that Mamata Banerjee has reshuffled leadership roles within the Trinamool Congress (TMC), removing Saayoni Ghosh and Mala Roy from key positions. Additionally, Kakoli Ghosh has faced public criticism after her son alleged that a TMC leader in Mamata Banerjee’s camp called her mother an alcoholic. The reports highlight internal party tensions and public backlash against TMC supporters.

The raids were conducted by law enforcement agencies investigating financial irregularities.

articles.title: JAO Lacked Jurisdiction to Issue Section 148 Notice: Rajasthan HC
articles.summary: The Rajasthan High Court ruled that the Joint Commissioner of Income Tax (JAO) lacked jurisdiction to issue a Section 148 notice. The court directed the appellate authority to decide the matter expeditiously. This decision has implications for tax notices and the power of tax authorities in India.
articles.raw_summary: While recognising that earlier judgments had invalidated JAO-issued notices, the Court avoided passing orders that would make the pending appeal redundant. Instead, it directed the appellate authority to decide the matter expeditiously. The post JAO Lacked Jurisdiction to Issue Section 148 Notice: Rajasthan HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Directs GP-Based Addition as Jewellery Purchases Formed Part of Sales & Closing Stock
articles.summary: The Delhi ITAT held that where purchases are reflected in accepted sales and closing stock, the entire purchase amount cannot be disallowed. The Tribunal directed the Assessing Officer to restrict the addition to the gross profit element by applying the average GP ratio of the preceding five years. The post ITAT Directs GP-Based Addition as Jewellery Purchases Formed Part of Sales & Closing Stock appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Tata's iPhone parts factory contaminated farmland water, India pollution body alleges
articles.summary: An Indian pollution regulator has warned Tata Electronics, a key supplier for Apple's iPhones, about a potential factory shutdown. The regulator alleges wastewater from the Tata plant in Hosur has contaminated groundwater near farms. Inspections found the factory discharged wastewater into a pond that overflowed. Tata claims an independent analysis shows full compliance with regulations.

Tata claims an independent analysis shows full compliance with regulations.

articles.title: India's Russian oil imports rise in May as refiners boost purchases
articles.summary: India continued its strong demand for Russian fossil fuels in May. The nation emerged as the second-largest buyer globally, importing hydrocarbons worth 5.8 billion euros. Crude oil formed the bulk of these imports, with refiners significantly increasing their purchases from Russia. This trend highlights India's strategic energy sourcing amidst global shifts.

articles.title: Latent Bars To Justice: An Empirical Analysis Of Bail
articles.summary: Any member of a legally civilised society can reasonably make this presupposition that law is inherently just and that eventually justice will be served. The law is in a continuous pursuit of justice. The only impediment to the conclusion of this pursuit is the human element. Anthropogenic impediments like corruption, inordinate delays, etc., stain the law and stand in the way of achieving justice. But let's suppose humans were inherently devoid of malice and motivated to follow the law. What if

articles.title: Madras HC Orders Fresh Review of Chartered Shipbrokers’ Section 2(15) Tax Exemption Status
articles.summary: The Madras High Court has ordered a fresh review of the tax exemption status of chartered shipbrokers under Section 2(15) of the Income-tax Act. The court's decision comes after the Supreme Court's guidance on general public utility institutions. This ruling may have significant implications for the tax exemption status of chartered shipbrokers in India.
articles.raw_summary: Referring to the Supreme Court's guidance on general public utility institutions, the Court observed that entities engaged in professional or trade-related activities warrant detailed scrutiny. The case was remanded for re-examination under the revised legal standards. articles.title: Gauhati HC Quashes GST Cancellation as Order Contained No Reasons
articles.summary: The Gauhati High Court quashed the cancellation of a GST registration due to a lack of reasons in the order. The court held that cancellation through a non-speaking order violates the statutory requirement of recording reasons. The matter has been remanded for fresh consideration.

articles.summary: The Tribunal held that preference share capital cannot be treated as unexplained cash credit once the assessee establishes identity, creditworthiness, and genuineness of investors. articles.title: Gauhati HC Quashes ITC Demand as Bona Fide Purchaser Cannot Be Penalised for Supplier’s Tax Default
articles.summary: The Gauhati High Court has quashed a demand for Input Tax Credit (ITC) from a purchasing dealer who acted in good faith and complied with statutory requirements. The court held that a bona fide purchaser cannot be penalized for the supplier's tax default. This ruling has implications for businesses in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that where a purchasing dealer has complied with statutory requirements and acted in good faith, ITC cannot be denied solely because the supplier failed to discharge tax obligations. The impugned adjudication and appellate orders were therefore quashed. The post Gauhati HC Quashes ITC Demand as Bona Fide Purchaser Cannot Be Penalised for Supplier s Tax Default appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: SC Rejects Review Liberty as Subsequent Judgments Are Not Grounds for Review
articles.summary: The Supreme Court rejected a review petition, holding that subsequent judgments cannot justify review of concluded cases. This decision is significant for its impact on the legal system and the power of the judiciary. The court relied on Order XLVII Rule 1 of the Code of Civil Procedure to dismiss the review petition.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that a later judgment modifying the legal position cannot, by itself, justify review of concluded cases. Relying on Order XLVII Rule 1 CPC, it dismissed the review petition and refused liberty to seek review. The post SC Rejects Review Liberty as Subsequent Judgments Are Not Grounds for Review appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Income Tax Notice Invalid for Failure to Follow Faceless Assessment Procedure
articles.summary: The High Court held that reassessment notices issued physically by jurisdictional officers instead of faceless authorities violate Section 151A. The ruling sets aside non-compliant notices while tying the final outcome to pending Supreme Court decisions. The post Income Tax Notice Invalid for Failure to Follow Faceless Assessment Procedure appeared first on TaxGuru .
articles.raw_summary: The High Court held that reassessment notices issued physically by jurisdictional officers instead of faceless authorities violate Section 151A. articles.title: A Successful Resolution Applicant Cannot Escape Through A 'Conditional' Letter Of Intent
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that a successful resolution applicant cannot withdraw or modify a resolution plan once it has been approved by the committee of creditors and sent to the adjudicating authority. This decision affects the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016. The ruling aims to ensure that resolution plans are implemented as intended.
articles.raw_summary: For some years, the position under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 has been settled. A successful resolution applicant cannot withdraw or modify a resolution plan once the committee of creditors (CoC) has approved it and the plan has gone to the adjudicating authority. The Supreme Court said as much in Ebix Singapore (P) Ltd. v. articles.title: Is India's economic growth real? Raghuram Rajan says something is off
articles.summary: Former RBI Governor Raghuram Rajan questions India's strong economic growth figures, citing weak corporate investment and declining foreign capital inflows. He argues these factors are difficult to reconcile with official data, suggesting a disconnect between headline numbers and business behavior. Rajan also highlights the lack of a clear economic roadmap and warns of vulnerabilities from the Middle East conflict.

articles.title: ‘Bring back his mortal remains’: Wife of Indian seafarer who died aboard vessel in Oman seeks ‘justice’
articles.summary: A grieving family is desperately seeking the repatriation of Indian seafarer Nishanth Uirthanathan's body from Oman and a thorough investigation into his death aboard MT Celestial. They allege a severe lack of communication from the shipping company and inadequate medical care. The Indian Embassy in Muscat is coordinating efforts for the body's return.

articles.title: India does not use methodology changes to inflate growth numbers, CEA Nageswaran defends GDP data
articles.summary: Chief Economic Adviser V Anantha Nageswaran defended India's GDP statistics, stating the country does not artificially inflate growth figures through methodological changes. He emphasized that India follows internationally accepted practices and that revisions, like the recent rebasing, demonstrate transparency rather than manipulation. Nageswaran suggested criticism often stems from unmet expectations rather than data quality concerns.

Investors are keenly watching the US-Iran deal's progress, which could impact global oil prices and market sentiment. Foreign investor outflows also remain a significant concern.

articles.title: Ashwini Vaishnaw expects more companies to start production of memory chips in India
articles.summary: Strong demand for memory (data storage) cards and advanced chips has tightened global supplies and supported higher prices in past quarters, and manufacturers have been sprucing up investments and production capacities to meet market requirements worldwide. The higher memory chip prices have, in turn, led to a rise in production costs for a range of electronic products, including smartphones and laptops.

articles.title: India's productivity gap with China widens despite strong GDP growth; manufacturing leap still missing: Report
articles.summary: India's labour productivity gap with China has widened significantly since 2000, despite strong economic growth. The country has yet to achieve an industry-led productivity transformation seen in peers like China and South Korea. Structural constraints, economic disruptions, and the pandemic have hampered progress, with a need for deeper reforms beyond current initiatives.

articles.title: RBI pushes banks to bring more dollars home
articles.summary: The push comes amid RBI measures to incentivise foreign currency inflows, including swap facilities and hedging cost support for FCNR(B) deposits, aimed at strengthening forex reserves and easing pressures on the rupee. The rupee depreciated nearly 11% last fiscal year and touched a record low of 96.96 against the US dollar in May. It closed at 95.11 on Friday.

articles.title: FPI exodus continues, Rs 62,800 cr pulled out from equities in first fortnight of June
articles.summary: Foreign investors continue to sell Indian shares. They have withdrawn over Rs 62,853 crore in early June. This follows significant outflows in previous months. Geopolitical tensions and global growth worries are driving these sales. The weakening rupee also plays a role. However, the pace of selling eased recently. Investors are seeking safety in developed markets.

Investors are seeking safety in developed markets.

articles.title: Dismissal Is Severest Punishment; Must Be Imposed Only For Grave Misconduct : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that dismissal from service is a severe punishment and should only be imposed after considering relevant factors. This decision aims to ensure that disciplinary actions are fair and just. The court also clarified that suspension pending inquiry cannot be imposed as a second punishment.

The government's approval was granted on June 3.

articles.title: ₹46,000 crore coal gasification scheme to drive industrial growth and Atmanirbhar Bharat, says Kishan Reddy
articles.summary: The Centre is stepping up efforts to accelerate coal gasification projects as part of a broader strategy to strengthen domestic manufacturing, reduce reliance on imported industrial feedstocks, and build resilient supply chains. Government officials highlighted policy support, assured coal linkages, and emerging indigenous technologies as key enablers for attracting long-term investments into the sector.

Government officials highlighted policy support, assured coal linkages, and emerging indigenous technologies as key enablers for attracting long-term investments into the sector.

articles.title: Need to raise private investment in India's space sector by changing mindset: Jitendra Singh
articles.summary: Union minister Jitendra Singh on Thursday said there was a need to change the mindset and scale up private investment as well as participation in India's space sector to make it more competitive.

articles.title: Prajwala's Consent Is Sovereign Until Someone Doubts It
articles.summary: The Supreme Court correctly placed a sex worker's consent at the centre of rescue and rehabilitation decisions. But having declared that consent unverifiable by any outsider, it tasked outsiders with verifying it. The structure that results does not make her consent sovereign, it makes it the first entry on a form someone else completes. The praise for Prajwala v. articles.title: Need For Thermal And Ergonomic Mandates In India's New Labour Codes
articles.summary: Noida witnessed over 40,000 garment workers' protest in April, 2026. This was not just attributed to wages, but also over the physical impossibility to survive a 12-hour work shift in temperatures reaching 42 degrees celsius. India has now operationalised its four new labour codes, consolidating 29 statutes. articles.title: Commencing Business Before Filing INC-20A: ROC imposes ₹3.5 Lakh Penalty
articles.summary: ROC held that starting business operations before filing the mandatory declaration under Section 10A violated the Companies Act. The company and its directors were penalized for the 179-day delay in compliance. The post Commencing Business Before Filing INC-20A: ROC imposes ₹3.5 Lakh Penalty appeared first on TaxGuru .
articles.raw_summary: ROC held that starting business operations before filing the mandatory declaration under Section 10A violated the Companies Act. The company and its directors were penalized for the 179-day delay in compliance. The post Commencing Business Before Filing INC-20A: ROC imposes ₹3.5 Lakh Penalty appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Minimum Alternate Tax (MAT) & Alternate Minimum Tax (AMT)
articles.summary: The Indian government has outlined the rules for Minimum Alternate Tax (MAT) and Alternate Minimum Tax (AMT) to ensure companies and eligible non-corporate taxpayers pay a minimum level of income tax. This move aims to prevent tax evasion and ensure fair taxation. The rules will apply to companies and taxpayers who claim deductions and exemptions.
articles.raw_summary: The document outlines how MAT and AMT ensure that companies and eligible non-corporate taxpayers pay a minimum level of income tax despite claiming deductions and exemptions. It explains the applicability, computation methods, credit mechanisms, exceptions, and compliance requirements governing these alternative tax provisions under the Income-tax Act. The post Minimum Alternate Tax (MAT) Alternate Minimum Tax (AMT) appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ‘50-year demand’ nears fulfilment as Zojila tunnel breakthrough brings Ladakh closer to all-weather connectivity
articles.summary: The Zojila tunnel, a key infrastructure project in Ladakh, has achieved a breakthrough after a 50-year demand for all-weather connectivity. The tunnel, connecting Srinagar to Leh, is being developed by the Border Roads Organisation (BRO) under the Ministry of Defence. This project aims to improve year-round access to Ladakh, which is currently cut off during winter due to heavy snowfall. The completion of the tunnel is expected to boost regional connectivity and strategic mobility.

articles.summary: Ola and Uber cab services in the Chandigarh-Mohali-Panchkula region were disrupted as drivers launched a strike protesting against their working conditions and demands for policy changes. The protest, which began on June 10, is set to continue until June 16. The drivers' demands include better earnings, fair working conditions, and regulatory protections for gig workers. The strike highlights ongoing concerns about the rights and welfare of platform-based workers in India.

articles.title: Odisha SHG Sends 7.5 Quintals of Amrapali Mangoes to London, Showcasing Odisha’s Global Agri Reach

This showcases Odisha’s growing agricultural export capabilities and the role of grassroots women’s groups in global markets. The initiative reflects broader trends in women’s empowerment and agri-business expansion in India.

articles.title: DRP 080626: Ramsar Milestone Masks Deepening Wetland Crisis in India
articles.summary: In Rajasthan, Sambhar Lake which was declared a Ramsar site in March 1990 has already lost nearly 89% of its wetland area due to illegal salt pans, groundwater extraction and unchecked development, raising fears that it may lose its wetland character altogether. articles.title: NLC India and CSIR-CECRI collaborate on critical mineral extraction technologies
articles.summary: NLC India Ltd (NLCIL) and CSIR-CECRI will collaborate to study overburden materials and tailings from NLC’s mines to assess the recovery potential of rare earth elements and other trace elements. The collaboration aims to develop sustainable and economically viable technologies for recovering critical minerals from secondary resources.

articles.title: Explained: Why RBI’s FCNR(B) and ECB swap window could be a game changer for banks
articles.summary: RBI’s FCNR(B) and ECB swap windows aim to boost liquidity, stabilise the rupee and ease funding costs for banks. articles.title: DLI-backed chip companies call for government procurement support
articles.summary: Indian semiconductor startups are urging the government for stronger market access and intellectual property protection. They highlight the need for procurement support to foster growth and maintain domestic ownership. Existing schemes have boosted design capabilities, but incentives for electronics makers to adopt Indian chips are lacking. Founders emphasize that capital availability, not just nationalism, drives ownership decisions.

articles.summary: Ather Energy's board has greenlit a substantial fundraising plan, aiming to secure up to Rs 2,500 crore. This significant capital infusion will be achieved through a combination of equity and convertible securities, including a Rs 1,500 crore Qualified Institutional Placement and Rs 1,000 crore from various other instruments.

articles.title: Meta Platforms hit by outage as Instagram, Facebook see surge in user complaints
articles.summary: Meta Platforms experienced a significant outage on Thursday evening, impacting both Instagram and Facebook. Users reported widespread issues with accessing feeds, posting content, and loading the applications. The outage may lead to discussions about the reliability and security of social media platforms.

articles.title: Google says ShinyHunters hackers targeting education sector via Oracle exploit
articles.summary: Google has reported that hackers known as ShinyHunters are targeting the education sector using an Oracle exploit. This incident may lead to policy action to improve cybersecurity in the education sector. The exploit affects PeopleSoft, an enterprise resource planning suite used by organizations to manage core business functions.

articles.title: Zelle to expand peer-to-peer payment service to India this year
articles.summary: Zelle, a popular payment service, is set to launch in India later this year. This marks Zelle's first international expansion. The company will also introduce its own U.S. dollar-backed stablecoin, ZelleUSD. This stablecoin will support Zelle's future international market growth. Zelle has seen significant transaction volume in the U.S. despite facing scrutiny over fraud.

articles.summary: India is now a global leader in agriculture, achieving food self-sufficiency. Foodgrain production has significantly increased, and agri exports are soaring. India is the world's largest rice exporter. The agricultural sector shows resilience and growth. Government focus and technological advancements are key drivers for meeting future food demands.

articles.title: RBI plans simpler cross-border payment approvals to boost MSMEs, exporters: EY report
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is looking to simplify cross-border payment approvals and reduce regulatory friction for businesses and exporters as part of its Payments Vision 2028, according to an EY report, which identified cross-border payments as a key strategic priority for the next phase of India's digital payments growth.

articles.title: Corporate affairs secy exhorts insolvency professionals to fast-track resolution
articles.summary: Corporate affairs secretary Deepti Gaur Mukerjee has urged insolvency professionals to fast-track resolution and prevent liquidation. She emphasized the importance of maintaining professional integrity and exhausting all avenues to revive distressed firms. This move aims to rescue distressed firms and prevent liquidation.
articles.raw_summary: Corporate affairs secretary Deepti Gaur Mukerjee urged insolvency professionals to "push boundaries of corporate turnaround" to rescue distressed firms and prevent liquidation. She emphasized maintaining utmost professional integrity and exhausting all avenues to revive entities, highlighting that the code of conduct is a "non-negotiable ethical anchor" for the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code's success.

articles.title: Farm credit demand likely to stay strong in FY27 on higher input costs, agri investments: NABARD
articles.summary: Farm credit demand is expected to remain strong in FY27 due to rising input costs and increased investments in agriculture. This is driven by factors such as expanded Kisan Credit Card coverage and higher investments in allied activities and infrastructure. The demand is expected to be strong, but higher borrowing costs pose a challenge for farmers and rural MSMEs.
articles.raw_summary: Agricultural and rural credit demand is projected to remain strong through 2026-27, driven by rising input costs, expanded Kisan Credit Card coverage, and increased investments in allied activities and infrastructure. NABARD Deputy Managing Director Ajay Kumar Sood noted a significant shift towards long-term investment credit, though higher borrowing costs pose a challenge for farmers and rural MSMEs.

articles.title: India's FTAs set stage for $1 trillion export target: Report
articles.summary: India's new generation of Free Trade Agreements can act as a catalyst for manufacturing expansion, private capex revival and supply-chain integration, with Electronics, Pharmaceuticals and Engineering   Machinery Goods positioned as the strongest beneficiaries. Yes Securities said the agreements, combined with PLI schemes and "China+1" diversification, give India its clearest shot yet at achieving US$1 trillion in merchandise exports by 2030.
articles.raw_summary: India's new generation of Free Trade Agreements can act as a catalyst for manufacturing expansion, private capex revival and supply-chain integration, with Electronics, Pharmaceuticals and Engineering Machinery Goods positioned as the strongest beneficiaries. articles.title: Inflation in June quarter likely to undershoot RBI forecast, rate hike probability drops ahead of monsoon risks
articles.summary: India's inflation is giving the Reserve Bank of India room to hold interest rates through the first quarter of fiscal year 2027. However, risks from food and fuel prices could change this outlook later in the year. The central bank will closely watch economic data before making any decisions on interest rates.

articles.title: Retail inflation edges up to 3.9% in May
articles.summary: Retail inflation in India rose to 3.9% in May. Economists expect price pressures to intensify further in June due to supply disruptions. This may lead to policy action in the future.
articles.raw_summary: Economists expect price pressures to intensify further in June, driven by the continued pass-through of earlier increases in transport and cooking fuel costs amid continued supply disruptions due to the West Asia conflict.

articles.title: Weak monsoon may drag agri GDP into contraction
articles.summary: Indian agriculture is at a crossroads, facing significant hurdles in the coming years. Analysts project little to no growth for agricultural GDP in 2026-27, compounded by El Nino fears pushing farmers to adopt less water-reliant crops. While this could alleviate water scarcity, it risks diminishing farm income and curtailing rural consumer activity.

articles.title: Call to extend duty cuts on critical inputs soon
articles.summary: India is set to decide on extending customs duty relief for critical raw material imports beyond June 30. An inter-ministerial panel will review easing import curbs on key inputs and consider fresh duties on certain locally manufactured electronics. The government is also evaluating duty cut requests from the pharmaceutical and steel sectors.

articles.title: El Nino sets in, clouds kharif outlook
articles.summary: El Nino conditions have set in and may strengthen during the southwest monsoon, raising concerns for India's largest sowing window. The neutral Indian Ocean Dipole offers no offset, and the IMD has lowered its monsoon forecast to below-normal. This development threatens crop output, impacting inflation and the rural economy.

articles.title: Govt hikes onion procurement rate to Rs 16.5 per kg from Saturday to help farmers
articles.summary: The government has increased the onion procurement price to Rs 16.50 per kg. This move aims to boost farmers' income through the buffer stock programme. Farmers in Maharashtra are seeking a higher rate of Rs 30 per kg. The government has set a procurement target of 2 lakh tonnes for the year.

articles.title: Assam CM flags off international consignment of GI-tagged Tezpur litchis to Singapore
articles.summary: Assam's Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma has launched the export of GI-tagged Tezpur litchis to Singapore, marking a significant step for the state's farming community. This follows a successful export to Dubai and signifies growing international demand. The government is strengthening market linkages and export infrastructure.

articles.title: RBI forex measures to lower banks' overseas borrowing costs by 2-2.5 per cent, aid inflows: Report
articles.summary: New RBI measures aim to make overseas borrowing cheaper for banks. External commercial borrowing costs may drop by up to 2.50 percent. This will help banks raise funds at lower rates and boost liquidity. Special swap facilities will also cut hedging costs. These steps are expected to strengthen India's foreign exchange reserves and ease deposit mobilization pressures for banks.

articles.title: India's forex reserves drop $711 million to $681.610 billion, RBI data shows
articles.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves saw a dip of $711 million, settling at $681.610 billion by June 5. This decline was primarily driven by a significant drop in foreign currency assets, which fell by $2.704 billion. However, the value of gold reserves experienced an increase of $1.975 billion during the same period.

articles.summary: India's retail inflation rose to 3.93% in May. Food and fuel prices increased, impacting consumer costs. This marks the highest inflation reading under the new CPI series. Despite the rise, inflation stays below the Reserve Bank of India's 4% target for the 16th month. Policymakers have room to support growth.

articles.summary: Chhattisgarh secured investment MoUs exceeding Rs 9,500 crore from seven companies. This initiative is projected to generate more than 7,800 employment opportunities. articles.title: India willing to let fiscal deficit widen to 4.8% of GDP: Report
articles.summary: India faces a larger budget deficit this year. The war in Iran is increasing energy subsidy costs. This puts pressure on government finances. Authorities may allow the deficit to widen by half a percentage point. The deficit could reach 4.8% of GDP. This is up from the 4.3% target set in February. The situation is being closely watched.

articles.title: BIS sets norms to protect indigenous seeds
articles.summary: The Bureau of Indian Standards has introduced a new framework for community seed banks. This standard aims to conserve India's traditional seed varieties. Farmers and local groups can now manage and exchange locally adapted seeds more effectively.

articles.title: Gearing up for El Nino: NCCF puts tenders to sell Tur Dal on hold
articles.summary: The central government has paused tenders for selling tur pulses. This decision follows a request from the All India Dal Millers Association. They want the government to wait for two months. This waiting period will allow for clarity on rainfall and sowing. The industry fears low production due to El Nino.

This strategy leverages India's young talent and competitive costs with developed economies' capital and technology. Recent free trade agreements with 38 countries highlight this approach. These collaborations open new markets and foster integration into global value chains. India's growing economy and large consumer base attract significant investment, creating jobs and driving innovation.

articles.title: India to remain fastest-growing major economy at 6.6 pc in FY27, down from 7.2 last year: World Bank
articles.summary: India will remain the world's fastest-growing major economy. Growth is projected to slow to 6.6 percent in fiscal year 2026-27. This moderation is due to higher energy prices and input costs. However, the economy is expected to rebound to 7.2 percent growth in fiscal 2027-28. Domestic demand remains robust, supporting economic activity.

It includes a four-lane highway with bridges, underpasses, and safety features.

articles.title: Credit guarantee scheme for MFI 2.0 gets extension
articles.summary: The Credit Guarantee Scheme for Microfinance Institutions-2.0 has been extended. This extension will last until August or until guarantees worth ₹20,000 crore are issued. articles.title: Ujjwala Yojana: Subsidised LPG cylinders capped at 4
articles.summary: The government has reduced the number of subsidized LPG cylinders under the Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) from nine to four per year for beneficiaries. PMUY, launched in 2016, provides free LPG connections to adult women from poor households. This change impacts millions of households relying on subsidized fuel for cooking. The decision follows earlier reductions from 12 to nine cylinders last year.
articles.raw_summary: Under the Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY), launched in May 2016 to provide deposit-free LPG connections to adult women from poor households, beneficiaries were initially entitled to 12 subsidised 14.2-kg cylinders a year. The subsidised quota was reduced to nine cylinders last year and has now been cut further to four.

articles.title: CAG audit of Panchayati Raj Institutions to improve their functioning, ease of living: CEA
articles.summary: The Chief Economic Advisor (CEA) Nageswaran highlighted the need for a Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) audit of Panchayati Raj Institutions to improve their functioning and ease of living. He also proposed introducing uniform accounting heads for fiscal accountability of states, ensuring consistent treatment of central transfers to local bodies for better comparability and transparency.
articles.raw_summary: Nageswaran also said there is a need to introduce uniform accounting heads for fiscal accountability of states, ensuring homogeneous treatment of all central transfers to local bodies to enable comparability.

articles.title: Can AI plan a cheaper vacation than a travel agent?
articles.summary: An experiment pitting AI against a seasoned travel agent for a Delhi-Manali trip revealed surprising similarities in itinerary planning. articles.title: PR Krishnamoorthy, the corporate conscience of an earlier India
articles.summary: Pudhucode Ramayyar Krishnamoorthy, a former Executive Director and Company Secretary of The Times of India Group, passed away at 92. He mentored many, leaving a legacy of rigor and integrity.

articles.summary: BRICS nations have adopted the "Indore Declaration," pledging enhanced cooperation in agriculture and launching new initiatives like a global forum on farmers' seed rights and a digital agriculture network. articles.title: Hardeep Singh Puri says India has over 60 days of crude, gas stocks; rules out any energy supply crunch
articles.summary: India assures ample energy supplies, with over 60 days of crude oil and natural gas reserves. Domestic LPG production has significantly reduced import reliance, ensuring 75-80 days of stock. Despite global price hikes due to the Iran conflict, India has kept domestic fuel prices stable, even seeing a slight decrease due to excise duty cuts.

The minister also addressed El Nino concerns, highlighting adequate government preparations and international cooperation.
